description: A White House-branded line-chart infographic on a white background titled in navy serif type 'Total Native-Born Employment Level'. A legend shows two colored series: a navy-blue line labeled 'Biden Administration' and a red line labeled 'Trump Administration (so far)'. The y-axis is labeled 'Total employment (thousands)' running from 129,000 to 134,000; the x-axis runs from Nov. 2023 to Nov. 2025. A dashed vertical line marks 'Inauguration' (early 2025). The blue (Biden) segment dips and stays roughly flat around 130,000-131,000, while the red (Trump) segment rises sharply to about 133,000+. A dark navy footer bar reads 'THE WHITE HOUSE / WASHINGTON'. The chart is designed to visually dramatize a claim that native-born employment surged under Trump. The tweet asserts 'Under President Trump, native-born Americans have accounted for ALL NET JOB GAINS. PROMISES MADE, PROMISES KEPT!' The propaganda technique is selective data framing tied to an immigration/jobs narrative favoring native-born workers. Target audience: economically-focused supporters.
notable_text: Total Native-Born Employment Level; Biden Administration vs Trump Administration (so far); Inauguration; THE WHITE HOUSE
